Fig. 3. Performance comparisons among algorithms for different number of RSUs

The proposed LAMA-GAP outperforms the two baseline algorithms for all cases. For example, for the number of RSUs is 9, the maximum load of RSUs for LAMA-GAP is 1.6226, for BA-MA is 2.4225, for BA-GR is 2.3111. In other words, the maximum load of RSUs for LAMA-GAP is lower by 33.1% and 29.8%, compared with that for BA-MA and BA-GR. Besides, the performance of all algorithms improves as the number of RSUs increases. This is because a higher number of RSUs provides more available resources, allowing tasks to have more RSU options, which in turn reduces the maximum load of RSUs.

Fig. 4 shows the performance comparisons between the proposed algorithm LAMA-GAP and baseline algorithm BA-MA and BA-GR, in terms of the maximum load of RSUs, for different numbers of RSUs with GPUs. As shown in Fig. 4, LAMA-GAP outperforms BA-MA and BA-GR for all cases in terms of the maximum load of RSUs. For example, for the case of the number of RSUs with GPU is 5, our LAMA-GAP reduces the maximum load of RSUs by 44.5% and 42.9% compared to BA-MA and BA-GR, respectively.
